feedback_summary: Baseline artifacts implement the required per-record QC computations, intermediate columns, margin formula, verdicts, and sensitivity analysis and pass the deterministic artifact-contract precheck. Two minor reproducibility/documentation gaps remain: (1) no separate runnable notebook/script artifact was provided (only a code snippet in methods_note.md), and (2) methods_note.md lacks an explicit tie-breaking rule for equal weighted driver_term values. Additionally, the CSV does not include per-record provenance columns that cite the original embedded-packet field keys used to generate each metric/limit decision.

deterministic_precheck.notes: Deterministic precheck passed; artifacts present and parsable.

suggested_fixes_summary: Add runnable notebook/script that reproduces CSV from embedded packet.
Document deterministic tie-break rule in methods_note.md and include a unit-test/example.
Add provenance columns to calcium_deconvolution_qc.csv and mention them in methods_note.md.
extra_model_fields.verifier_notes: Verifier concurs with grader: two minor issues remain (runnable artifact and tie-break rule).

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

actionable_feedback.how_to_fix: Add a runnable Jupyter notebook or a single Python script that ingests the embedded packet and produces [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] Include a short README that explains how to run it (requirements, commands).
actionable_feedback.why: Without an executable notebook or script, full end-to-end reproducibility cannot be validated even though the CSV and methods note provide formulas and a snippet.

actionable_feedback.how_to_fix: Add a deterministic tie-break rule to methods_note.md (for example: when driver_term values tie, select the driver based on lexicographic ordering of metric name; include an explicit sentence and a one-row example/test demonstrating the rule).
actionable_feedback.issue: Tie-breaking rule for equal weighted driver_term values not documented
actionable_feedback.why: The rubric requires an explicit tie-breaking rule even if current rows do not exercise it. Omitting it leaves an edge-case unspecified.

actionable_feedback.how_to_fix: Add provenance columns to the CSV (e.g., source_metric1_field, source_metric2_field, source_metric3_field, source_metric4_field, limits_source) populated with the original embedded-packet keys or a short pointer string. Also mention these columns in methods_note.md.
actionable_feedback.issue: Per-record provenance not present in CSV
actionable_feedback.why: Auditability requires each CSV row to cite the exact embedded-packet field keys or parameter names used to compute metrics/limits so future reviewers can trace numbers back to source keys.

artifact_specific_comments.comment: Comprehensive and correctly documents formulas and sensitivity analysis. Add explicit tie-break rule and clarify the warn-rule phrasing to remove ambiguity ('warn if margin >= 0 and margin < 0.08*largest_driver'). Add a short section referencing the runnable artifact (path and usage).

Contains all intermediate computed columns and sensitivity shifts as required. Add per-record provenance columns (source field names) and, if possible, a provenance checksum or pointer back to the embedded packet.
